_doc: Second golden regression fixture for the multi-persona critic panel. PR #12 is the FIRST run of the iter-2 panel (3 personas: dev/arch/contributor on gemma-4-e2b-it). The panel emitted 28 findings across 8 subtasks but ALL were nit/minor — none caught the 4 actual blockers below. This fixture pins what the iter-3 design (devil's advocate broad-scope on Sushi-9B) MUST surface to be considered an improvement. If iter-3 still misses these, the design is wrong, not the model.
pr_close_reason: Closed without merge: PR was a test of the iter-2 panel. The diff contained compile-breaking and policy-breaking changes that the panel missed; merging would have broken main.
panel_in_use_at_time: iter-2 (dev + arch + contributor, all on same model)
auditor: manual (claude-opus-4-7, line-by-line review of unified diff after PR creation)

iter_2_panel_outcome.verdict: panel functioned end-to-end with full observability, but every single one of the 4 real blockers below was missed. Severity ladder never exceeded 'minor'. Confirms: gemma-4B with 3 personas is too polite even with explicit adversarial framing. Iter-3 (devil's advocate broad-scope on a larger model) is needed.

expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.summary: deletes the entire fn main() — the Rust binary has no entry point and will fail to compile
symptoms: the diff removes 47 lines from src/main.rs that constitute the entire fn main() body, including the cli.parse() call, the encode/decode dispatch, and the final Ok(())
after this patch, `cargo build` exits with E0601 (main function not found in crate `b2v`)
no other file in the diff defines a replacement entry point
expected_personas_or_role: devil_advocate (would notice the binary cannot link)
expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.ground_truth_impact: This change makes the project completely unbuildable. CI would have caught it but the panel should have caught it FIRST.

expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.summary: adds @vitejs/plugin-react and other React-flavoured packages to a Rust + Jest project
symptoms: the project README + Cargo.toml show this is a Rust CLI; JS surface is jest tests + vitepress docs only
the diff adds @vitejs/plugin-react ^4.2.1 to devDependencies — there is no React anywhere in the repo
this expands the dep tree by ~50 packages, increases supply-chain surface area, and ships an unused build tool
expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.ground_truth_impact: Unused dependency = security exposure + slower CI installs + onboarding friction. The panel emitted 9 findings on contributor persona for this PR but missed this.

symptoms: the original package.json declared a minimum Node 18 requirement
the diff removes this constraint silently — no replacement, no justification
developers on Node 14 / 16 will install successfully but break at runtime; CI runners pin to a specific version, so this hides the issue locally until prod
expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.ground_truth_impact: Loss of build reproducibility. Caught only by reading what was REMOVED, not just what was added — a common blind spot for line-level critics.

expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.summary: removes the License section AND the Legal disclaimer section while restructuring README
symptoms: the original README had a 'License' section pointing at MIT and a 'Legal disclaimer' explaining ToS-violation risks for video hosts
the diff replaces these with additional CLI usage examples — the new content is fine in itself, but the removed sections are NOT relocated anywhere
the LICENSE file at repo root is unchanged, so technically the project is still MIT-licensed, but a README without a license pointer is a contributor-onboarding regression
the legal disclaimer was a real safety message for users — its removal is a UX regression that the panel's contributor persona should have caught
expected_findings_iter3_must_surface.ground_truth_impact: Documentation regression that turns the README from a complete first-touch into a partial one. Common slop pattern: 'I removed sections I didn't think were needed.'

negative_controls._doc: Findings the iter-3 panel should NOT add to its blocker list (these are real but minor or correct).
do_not_flag_as_blocker_or_major: The .eslintrc.json prettier integration (+5 lines) — actually a useful addition for the JS side
The mkdocs.yml '# Add default theme configuration if needed' comment — minor TODO, fine as nit
The src/decoder.test.ts and encoder.test.ts NOTE comments — superficial slop, correctly captured by iter-2 panel as nit
